summary: Matched ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag by cleaning up the SDI-in-hitlag logic and propagating semantic SDI names through fighter/common-data structs and related guard/collision call sites. decomp.dev reported one new match: ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage from 86.07% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Target match; rewrote ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag and updated SDI threshold uses in damage/hitlag logic.
Renamed SDI-related ftCommonData fields and Fighter bitfield while preserving documented offsets.
Updated guard SDI/guard-setoff code to use allow_sdi and named SDI common-data fields.
Updated hitlag lifecycle code to set, clear, and test allow_sdi.
Updated collision-path branches that depend on the SDI-allowed state.
Updated C-stick SDI magnitude check to use sdi_min_stick_mag.

what_changed: Replaced the old SOLUTION/M2C_FIELD-heavy ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag body with a compact condition using GET_FIGHTER, allow_sdi, VEC2_SQ_LEN, SQ, and named p_ftCommonData SDI fields.
Changed the left-stick SDI eligibility check from hand-written squared-component arithmetic against x4B0 to VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) >= SQ(p_ftCommonData->sdi_min_stick_mag).

Computed scaled_lstick_x and scaled_lstick_y from fp->input.lstick and p_ftCommonData->sdi_pos_scale, applied them to fp->cur_pos, reset both stick tilt timers to 254, and called pl_800401F0 with fp->x221F_b4.
Renamed ftCommonData +4B0/+4B4/+4B8 from x4B0/x4B4/x4B8 to sdi_min_stick_mag/sdi_stick_window/sdi_pos_scale and updated uses in damage, guard, and C-stick checks.
Renamed Fighter bitfield fp+221A:2 from x221A_b2 to allow_sdi and updated hitlag, guard, and collision-path use sites.
Removed #define SOLUTION, #if SOLUTION alternatives, and raw M2C_FIELD accesses from the matched function.
smart_moves: Moved semantic names into types.h first-class fields, then reused those names across dependent files instead of leaving local aliases like cd_x4B0 or raw x221A_b2.
Removed conditional preprocessor scaffolding and raw-offset M2C_FIELD alternatives once the relevant Fighter fields were available as typed members.
Used existing vector/square macros, VEC2_SQ_LEN and SQ, to express the magnitude threshold in one condition and reduce temporary variables.
Kept the allow_sdi rename consistent across systems: damage hitlag handling, guard setoff physics/collision, fighter hitlag lifecycle, and ft_081B collision variants.
decomp_lessons: A target-function match can require semantic cleanup outside the function; this match involved types.h renames plus use-site updates in Guard, fighter.c, ft_081B, and ft_0DF1.
When common-data offsets are only named xNNNN but all uses line up around one mechanic, rename the struct fields to mechanic-specific names and update every consumer in the same PR.
For bitfields, preserve the exact offset/bit comment while replacing the member name; this keeps layout-sensitive Fighter work reviewable.
Prefer project accessors/macros such as GET_FIGHTER, VEC2_SQ_LEN, and SQ over ad-hoc gobj->user_data casts and hand-written arithmetic when they produce cleaner matching code.
Remove M2C_FIELD/raw-offset probes after identifying the typed member; lingering alternatives obscure final control flow and make the matched source harder to search.

naming_conventions.meaning: Bitfield gate for SDI availability during hitlag and related collision paths.
Minimum analog stick magnitude threshold for SDI-related checks.
Stick tilt timer/window used to allow repeated SDI movement.
Scale applied to stick input to compute SDI position displacement.

naming_conventions: Use lower_snake_case mechanic names for discovered ftCommonData fields and Fighter bitfields while keeping offset comments intact.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The final ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag used one compound if combining allow_sdi, VEC2_SQ_LEN >= SQ(sdi_min_stick_mag), and timer-window checks before applying displacement.
GET_FIGHTER(gobj) replaced direct gobj->user_data in the matched function.
VEC2_SQ_LEN(fp->input.lstick) and SQ(...) replaced local temporaries and explicit lstick_x * lstick_x + lstick_y * lstick_y code.
Raw M2C_FIELD references for fp+0x670, fp+0x671, and fp+0x221F were removed in favor of x670_timer_lstick_tilt_x, x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y, and x221F_b4.
The automated report confirms ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag reached 100.00% for the ftCo_Damage unit.
